that score-based merge sorts globally by score.\n        // With global IDF (simulated here by consistent scores across shards),\n        // doc with highest score should rank first regardless of shard.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                // Shard 0: low scores\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-1\", 0.3, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-low-2\", 0.2, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n                // Shard 1: high scores (these should rank higher)\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-1\", 0.9, 1),\n                        make_hit(\"doc-high-2\", 0.8, 1),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 4);\n\n        // Should be sorted by score descending globally\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-high-2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[2].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[3].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-low-2\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_tie_breaking() {\n        // Test deterministic tie-breaking on primary key when scores are equal.\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                make_shard_response(\n                    vec![\n                        make_hit(\"zebra\", 0.5, 0),\n                        make_hit(\"apple\", 0.5, 0),\n                    ],\n                    50,\n                    10,\n                ),\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        // Both docs have same score, should tie-break alphabetically\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"apple\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"zebra\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_offset_limit() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![\n                    make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc2\", 0.8, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc3\", 0.7, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc4\", 0.6, 0),\n                    make_hit(\"doc5\", 0.5, 0),\n                ],\n                100,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 1,\n            limit: 2,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc2\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc3\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_preserves_score_when_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.9)\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_strips_score_when_not_requested() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![make_shard_response(\n                vec![make_hit(\"doc1\", 0.9, 0)],\n                50,\n                10,\n            )],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").is_none());\n    }\n\n    /// Integration test: skewed corpus scenario with global-IDF preflight.\n    ///\n    /// This simulates the scenario described in P12.OP4:\n    /// - Shard 0 has 1000 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 100 (df=100)\n    /// - Shard 1 has 100 docs, term \"rust\" appears in 50 (df=50)\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF:\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 0) = log((1000-100+0.5)/(100+0.5)+1) ≈ 2.2\n    /// - Local IDF(shard 1) = log((100-50+0.5)/(50+0.5)+1) ≈ 0.7\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF:\n    /// - Global N = 1100, global df = 150\n    /// - Global IDF = log((1100-150+0.5)/(150+0.5)+1) ≈ 1.8\n    ///\n    /// A document with tf=3 for \"rust\" in each shard:\n    /// - Shard 0 score (local IDF): ~3 * 2.2 = 6.6\n    /// - Shard 1 score (local IDF): ~3 * 0.7 = 2.1\n    ///\n    /// Without global IDF, shard 0 doc ranks higher despite shard 1 having\n    /// much higher term density (50/100 vs 100/1000).\n    ///\n    /// With global IDF, both shards use IDF=1.8, and the doc with higher\n    /// term density (normalized by document length) ranks correctly.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_skewed_corpus_integration() {\n        // Simulate global IDF applied (scores are now comparable)\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Doc in large shard with high term frequency but low density\n        let doc_large_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-large\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.75, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // Doc in small shard with lower term frequency but high density\n        let doc_small_shard = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-small\",\n            \"title\": \"Rust in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.85, // After global-IDF normalization\n        });\n\n        // With global IDF, the small shard doc should rank higher\n        // because its term density is higher (50/100 vs 100/1000)\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_large_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_small_shard],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 2);\n\n        // Small shard doc should rank first (higher score after global IDF)\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-small\");\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[1].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-large\");\n\n        // Scores should be preserved\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[0].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.85)\n        );\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.hits[1].get(\"_rankingScore\").unwrap().as_f64(),\n            Some(0.75)\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Test that demonstrates the failure mode without global IDF.\n    ///\n    /// This shows what happens when scores are NOT comparable across shards:\n    /// score-based merge produces incorrect rankings, while RRF at least\n    /// produces consistent (though not optimal) results.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_without_global_idf_fails() {\n        // Simulate the bug: shard-local IDF produces incomparable scores\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n        // Shard 0: large shard, inflated local IDF (shard has term rarity)\n        let doc_shard0 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-inflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Large Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.95, // Inflated due to high local IDF\n        });\n\n        // Shard 1: small shard, deflated local IDF (term is common here)\n        let doc_shard1 = json!({\n            \"id\": \"doc-deflated\",\n            \"title\": \"Document in Small Shard\",\n            \"_rankingScore\": 0.60, // Deflated due to low local IDF\n        });\n\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard0],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 10000,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 15,\n                    }),\n                },\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": [doc_shard1],\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": 100,\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 5,\n                    }),\n                },\n            ],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: true,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n\n        // Without global IDF, score-based merge trusts the inflated scores\n        assert_eq!(result.hits[0].get(\"id\").unwrap(), \"doc-inflated\");\n\n        // This is WRONG: doc-deflated has much higher term density but\n        // ranks lower due to shard-local IDF skew.\n        //\n        // The solution is the preflight phase (dfs_query_then_fetch_search)\n        // which computes global IDF so scores are comparable.\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_default_impl() {\n        let via_default: ScoreMergeStrategy = Default::default();\n        let via_constructor = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        assert_eq!(via_default.name(), via_constructor.name());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_score_merge_empty_input() {\n        let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n        let input = MergeInput {\n            shard_hits: vec![],\n            offset: 0,\n            limit: 10,\n            client_requested_score: false,\n            facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n        };\n\n        let result = strategy.merge(input).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.hits.len(), 0);\n        assert_eq!(result.estimated_total_hits, 0);\n    }\n\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n    // P12.OP4 RRF skew validation\n    // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n    /// Validates the P12.OP4 finding: RRF merge with extreme shard skew\n    /// produces incorrect global rankings because it gives equal weight\n    /// to all shards regardless of their size.\n    ///\n    /// Scenario: 10 shards where shard 0 has 93K docs (93%) and shard 9\n    /// has 10 docs (0.01%). RRF assigns identical scores to rank-0 hits\n    /// from all shards, so a mediocre hit from the tiny shard ranks\n    /// equally with the best hit from the dominant shard.\n    ///\n    /// Benchmark result (10K queries, skewed corpus):\n    ///   Score merge:   τ = 0.79  (95% CI [0.787, 0.801]) — FAIL\n    ///   RRF merge:     τ = 0.14  (95% CI [0.134, 0.140]) — FAIL\n    ///   DFS preflight: τ = 0.98  (95% CI [0.982, 0.982]) — PASS\n    ///\n    /// Conclusion: RRF alone does NOT solve cross-shard comparability.\n    /// Global-IDF preflight (dfs_query_then_fetch) is required.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_equal_weight_problem() {\n        // Shard 0 (dominant): doc-best should be the global #1 result.\n        // It has the highest score and appears in the shard with 93% of docs.\n        let shard_dominant = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-best\", 0.95, 0),      // True global #1\n                make_hit(\"doc-good\", 0.90, 0),       // True global #2\n                make_hit(\"doc-ok\", 0.85, 0),         // True global #3\n                make_hit(\"doc-mediocre\", 0.70, 0),   // True global #4\n                make_hit(\"doc-weak\", 0.60, 0),       // True global #5\n            ],\n            93_000,\n            10,\n        );\n\n        // Shard 9 (tiny, 10 docs): due to local IDF skew, irrelevant docs\n        // can appear at rank 0 with inflated local scores.\n        let shard_tiny = make_shard_response(\n            vec![\n                make_hit(\"doc-irrelevant\", 0.98, 9), // Inflated local IDF → high score\n                make_hit(\"doc-noise\", 0.92, 9),\n            ],\n            10,\n            2,\n        );\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: vec![shard_dominant, shard_tiny],\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 10,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let ids: Vec<_> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()))\n            .collect();\n\n        // RRF gives equal rank weight to both shards.\n        // Rank 0 from dominant shard: 1/61 ≈ 0.0164\n        // Rank 0 from tiny shard:     1/61 ≈ 0.0164 (identical!)\n        //\n        // Tie-breaking falls to primary key (alphabetical), NOT relevance.\n        // doc-best and doc-irrelevant both get RRF score 1/61.\n        // Alphabetically: doc-best < doc-irrelevant → doc-best wins the tie.\n        //\n        // But doc-irrelevant still ranks above doc-good, doc-ok, doc-mediocre,\n        // and doc-weak — all of which are more relevant globally.\n        assert_eq!(ids[0], \"doc-best\");      // Tie-break win (alphabetical)\n        assert_eq!(ids[1], \"doc-irrelevant\"); // Tie-break loss, but still rank 2!\n\n        // doc-irrelevant (globally irrelevant) ranks ABOVE doc-good (global #2)\n        let irrelevant_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-irrelevant\").unwrap();\n        let good_pos = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == \"doc-good\").unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            irrelevant_pos < good_pos,\n            \"RRF skew bug: irrelevant doc (pos {}) ranks above doc-good (pos {})\",\n            irrelevant_pos,\n            good_pos,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Computes Kendall tau between two rankings (document ID lists).\n    /// Used to validate merge quality against ground truth.\n    fn kendall_tau(ranking1: &[String], ranking2: &[String]) -> f64 {\n        let pos1: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking1\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n        let pos2: std::collections::HashMap<&str, usize> = ranking2\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, id)| (id.as_str(), i))\n            .collect();\n\n        let common: Vec<&str> = pos1\n            .keys()\n            .filter(|k| pos2.contains_key(*k))\n            .map(|k| *k)\n            .collect();\n\n        if common.len() < 2 {\n            return 0.0;\n        }\n\n        let r2_positions: Vec<usize> = common.iter().map(|id| pos2[id]).collect();\n        let (_, discordant) = count_inversions(&r2_positions);\n        let n = common.len();\n        let total = n * (n - 1) / 2;\n        let concordant = total - discordant;\n        (concordant as f64 - discordant as f64) / total as f64\n    }\n\n    fn count_inversions(arr: &[usize]) -> (Vec<usize>, usize) {\n        if arr.len() <= 1 {\n            return (arr.to_vec(), 0);\n        }\n        let mid = arr.len() / 2;\n        let (left, inv_l) = count_inversions(&arr[..mid]);\n        let (right, inv_r) = count_inversions(&arr[mid..]);\n\n        let mut merged = Vec::with_capacity(arr.len());\n        let mut inv = inv_l + inv_r;\n        let (mut i, mut j) = (0, 0);\n\n        while i < left.len() && j < right.len() {\n            if left[i] <= right[j] {\n                merged.push(left[i]);\n                i += 1;\n            } else {\n                merged.push(right[j]);\n                inv += left.len() - i;\n                j += 1;\n            }\n        }\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&left[i..]);\n        merged.extend_from_slice(&right[j..]);\n        (merged, inv)\n    }\n\n    /// End-to-end validation: RRF merge on skewed shards produces τ < 0.95\n    /// against ground truth (single-index ranking).\n    ///\n    /// This is a scaled-down version of the 10K-query Python benchmark.\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rrf_skewed_shards_tau_below_threshold() {\n        let k = DEFAULT_RRF_K;\n\n        // Build 5 shards with skewed sizes: [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000]\n        // Ground truth: all 17600 docs in one index, sorted by score.\n        let mut all_docs: Vec<(String, f64)> = Vec::new();\n        let mut shard_docs: Vec<Vec<(String, f64)>> = vec![vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![], vec![]];\n        let shard_sizes = [100, 500, 2000, 5000, 10000];\n\n        let mut rng = simple_rng(42);\n        for (shard_id, &size) in shard_sizes.iter().enumerate() {\n            for i in 0..size {\n                // Deterministic pseudo-random scores\n                let score = fake_bm25_score(shard_id, i, &mut rng);\n                let doc_id = format!(\"s{}-d{:06}\", shard_id, i);\n                all_docs.push((doc_id.clone(), score));\n                shard_docs[shard_id].push((doc_id, score));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Ground truth: global sort by score descending\n        all_docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        let ground_truth: Vec<String> = all_docs.iter().take(100).map(|(id, _)| id.clone()).collect();\n\n        // Per-shard: sort locally (simulates local BM25 with local IDF)\n        for docs in &mut shard_docs {\n            docs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n        }\n\n        // RRF merge using the actual Rust merger\n        let shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage> = shard_docs\n            .iter()\n            .map(|docs| {\n                let hits: Vec<Value> = docs\n                    .iter()\n                    .take(200)\n                    .map(|(id, score)| {\n                        json!({\n                            \"id\": id,\n                            \"_rankingScore\": score,\n                        })\n                    })\n                    .collect();\n                ShardHitPage {\n                    body: json!({\n                        \"hits\": hits,\n                        \"estimatedTotalHits\": docs.len(),\n                        \"processingTimeMs\": 10,\n                    }),\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let strategy = RrfStrategy::new(k);\n        let result = strategy\n            .merge(MergeInput {\n                shard_hits: shard_pages,\n                offset: 0,\n                limit: 100,\n                client_requested_score: true,\n                facets: None,\n        failed_shards: Vec::new(),\n            })\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let rrf_ranking: Vec<String> = result\n            .hits\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|h| h.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).map(String::from))\n            .collect();\n\n        let tau = kendall_tau(&ground_truth, &rrf_ranking);\n\n        // RRF with skewed shards should produce τ well below 0.95.\n        assert!(\n            tau < 0.95,\n            \"RRF tau = {:.4}, expected < 0.95 with skewed shards\",\n            tau,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Simple deterministic PRNG for reproducible test scores.\n    fn simple_rng(seed: u64) -> impl FnMut() -> f64 {\n        let mut state = seed;\n        move || {\n            state = state.wrapping_mul(6_364_136_223_846_793_005).wrapping_add(1);\n            (state >> 33) as f64 / (1u64 << 31) as f64\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Simulates a BM25-like score with shard-dependent IDF skew.\n    fn fake_bm25_score(shard_id: usize, _doc_idx: usize, rng: &mut impl FnMut() -> f64) -> f64 {\n        let tf = 1.0 + rng() * 10.0;\n        // Larger shards have lower IDF for common terms (simulating skew)\n        let shard_weight = match shard_id {\n            0 => 0.3,\n            1 => 0.5,\n            2 => 0.7,\n            3 => 0.9,\n            4 => 1.0,\n            _ => 0.5,\n        };\n        tf * shard_weight + rng() * 0.5\n    }\n}\n","numLines":2018,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2018}}}

\"snake_case\")]\n18\tpub enum SelectionStrategy {\n19\t    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n20\t    Adaptive,\n21\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n22\t    RoundRobin,\n23\t    /// Random #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n44\t    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n45\t    /// Error rate weight.\n46\t    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n47\t    pub error_weight: metrics for adaptive selection.\n86\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n87\tpub struct NodeMetrics {\n88\t    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n89\t    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n90\t    /// Current in-flight request count.\n91\t    pub in_flight: u32,\n92\t    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n93\t    pub error_rate: f64,\n94\t    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n95\t    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n96\t    /// Last update timestamp.\n97\t    pub last_updated: Instant,\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\timpl NodeMetrics {\n101\t    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n102\t    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n103\t        Self {\n104\t            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n105\t            in_flight: 0,\n106\t            error_rate: 0.0,\n107\t            half_life_ms,\n108\t            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n109\t        }\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n113\t    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n114\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n115\t        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n116\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n120\t    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n121\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n122\t        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n123\t        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n124\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n125\t    }\n126\t\n127\t    /// Increment in-flight count.\n128\t    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n129\t        self.in_flight += 1;\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n133\t    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n134\t        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n135\t    }\n136\t\n137\t    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n138\t    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n139\t        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n140\t            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n141\t            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n142\t    }\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\timpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n146\t    fn default() -> Self {\n147\t        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Callback for reporting selection events.\n152\tpub trait SelectionObserver: Send + Sync {\n153\t    /// Called when a node is selected with its score.\n154\t    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64);\n155\t    /// Called when exploration selects a random node.\n156\t    fn report_exploration(&self);\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t/// No-op observer for when metrics aren't needed.\n160\tstruct NoOpObserver;\n161\timpl SelectionObserver for NoOpObserver {\n162\t    fn report_selection(&self, _node_id: &str, _score: f64) {}\n163\t    fn report_exploration(&self) {}\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\t/// Replica selector.\n167\tpub struct ReplicaSelector {\n168\t    /// Configuration.\n169\t    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n170\t    /// Per-node metrics.\n171\t    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n172\t    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n173\t    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n174\t    /// Random number generator.\n175\t    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n176\t    /// Observer for selection events.\n177\t    observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>,\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\timpl ReplicaSelector {\n181\t    /// Create a new replica selector with a metrics observer.\n182\t    pub fn new_with_observer(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig, observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>) -> Self {\n183\t        Self {\n184\t            config,\n185\t            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n186\t            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n187\t            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n188\t            observer,\n189\t        }\n190\t    }\n191\t\n192\t    /// Create a new replica selector without metrics.\n193\t    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n194\t        Self::new_with_observer(config, Arc::new(NoOpObserver))\n195\t    }\n196\t\n197\t    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n198\t    ///\n199\t    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n200\t    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n201\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n202\t            return None;\n203\t        }\n204\t\n205\t        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n206\t\n207\t        match strategy {\n208\t            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n209\t            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n210\t            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n211\t        }\n212\t    }\n213\t\n214\t    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n215\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n216\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n217\t\n218\t        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n219\t        if self.should_explore() {\n220\t            self.observer.report_exploration();\n221\t            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n222\t            if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n223\t                let score = metrics\n224\t                    .get(node)\n225\t                    .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n226\t                    .unwrap_or(1000.0);\n227\t                self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n228\t            }\n229\t            return selected;\n230\t        }\n231\t\n232\t        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the minimum score\n233\t        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n234\t        let mut best_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = Vec::new();\n235\t\n236\t        for node in candidates {\n237\t            let score = metrics\n238\t                .get(node)\n239\t                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n240\t                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n241\t\n242\t            if score < best_score {\n243\t                best_score = score;\n244\t                best_nodes.clear();\n245\t                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n246\t            } else if (score - best_score).abs() < 1e-10 {\n247\t                // Scores are essentially equal - add to tie list\n248\t                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n249\t            }\n250\t        }\n251\t\n252\t        // If multiple nodes have the same best score, pick randomly\n253\t        let selected = if best_nodes.len() == 1 {\n254\t            best_nodes.into_iter().next()\n255\t        } else {\n256\t            let idx = self.rng.lock().unwrap().gen_range(0..best_nodes.len());\n257\t            best_nodes.get(idx).cloned()\n258\t        };\n259\t\n260\t        if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n261\t            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        selected\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n268\t    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n269\t        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n270\t        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n271\t        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n272\t        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n273\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n274\t    }\n275\t\n276\t    /// Random selection.\n277\t    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n278\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n279\t            return None;\n280\t        }\n281\t        let idx = self\n282\t            .rng\n283\t            .lock()\n284\t            .unwrap()\n285\t            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n286\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n287\t    }\n288\t\n289\t    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n290\t    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n291\t        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n292\t        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n293\t    }\n294\t\n295\t    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n296\t    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n297\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n298\t        let entry = metrics\n299\t            .entry(node.clone())\n300\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n301\t        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n302\t        entry.update_error(false);\n303\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    /// Record a failed request.\n307\t    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n308\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n309\t        let entry = metrics\n310\t            .entry(node.clone())\n311\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n312\t        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n313\t            entry.update_latency(lat);\n314\t        }\n315\t        entry.update_error(true);\n316\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n317\t    }\n318\t\n319\t    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n320\t    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node: &NodeId) {\n321\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n322\t        let entry = metrics\n323\t            .entry(node.clone())\n324\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n325\t        entry.increment_in_flight();\n326\t    }\n327\t\n328\t    /// Get metrics for a node.\n329\t    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<NodeMetrics> {\n330\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n331\t        metrics.get(node).cloned()\n332\t    }\n333\t\n334\t    /// Parse the strategy from config string.\n335\t    fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n336\t        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n337\t            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n338\t            \"random\" => SelectionStrategy::Random,\n339\t            _ => SelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n340\t        }\n341\t    }\n342\t}\n343\t\n344\timpl Clone for ReplicaSelector {\n345\t    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n346\t        Self {\n347\t            config: self.config.clone(),\n348\t            metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),\n349\t            rr_counter: Arc::clone(&self.rr_counter),\n350\t            rng: Arc::clone(&self.rng),\n351\t            observer: Arc::clone(&self.observer),\n352\t        }\n353\t    }\n354\t}\n355\t\n356\timpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n357\t    fn default() -> Self {\n358\t        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n359\t    }\n360\t}\n361\t\n362\t#[cfg(test)]\n363\tmod tests {\n364\t    use super::*;\n365\t\n366\t    #[test]\n367\t    fn test_config_default() {\n368\t        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n369\t        assert_eq!(config.strategy, \"adaptive\");\n370\t        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n371\t        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n372\t        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n373\t    }\n374\t\n375\t    #[test]\n376\t    fn test_node_metrics_score() {\n377\t        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(50.0, 5000);\n378\t        assert_eq!(metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default()), 50.0);\n379\t\n380\t        metrics.in_flight = 5;\n381\t        let score = metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n382\t        // 50 * 1.0 + 5 * 2.0 = 60\n383\t        assert_eq!(score, 60.0);\n384\t    }\n385\t\n386\t    #[test]\n387\t    fn test_node_metrics_ewma() {\n388\t        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(100.0, 1000); // Short half-life\n389\t\n390\t        metrics.update_latency(50.0);\n391\t        // Should move toward 50\n392\t        assert!(metrics.latency_p95_ms < 100.0 && metrics.latency_p95_ms > 40.0);\n393\t\n394\t        metrics.update_error(true);\n395\t        assert!(metrics.error_rate > 0.0);\n396\t\n397\t        metrics.update_error(false);\n398\t        // Error rate should decay\n399\t        let rate_before = metrics.error_rate;\n400\t        metrics.update_error(false);\n401\t        assert!(metrics.error_rate < rate_before);\n402\t    }\n403\t\n404\t    #[tokio::test]\n405\t    async fn test_select_adaptive() {\n406\t        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n407\t\n408\t        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n409\t        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n410\t\n411\t        // Seed metrics by recording successful requests\n412\t        selector.record_success(&node1, 10.0).await;\n413\t        selector.record_success(&node2, 100.0).await;\n414\t\n415\t        // Should select node-1 (lower score)\n416\t        let candidates = vec![node2.clone(), node1.clone()];\n417\t        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n418\t        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n419\t    }\n420\t\n421\t    #[tokio::test]\n422\t    async fn test_select_round_robin() {\n423\t        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n424\t            strategy: \"round_robin\".into(),\n425\t            ..Default::default()\n426\t        };\n427\t        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n428\t\n429\t        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n430\t        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n431\t\n432\t        let candidates = vec![node1.clone(), node2.clone()];\n433\t\n434\t        // First call should return node-1\n435\t        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n436\t        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1.clone()));\n437\t\n438\t        // Second call should return node-2\n439\t        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n440\t        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node2.clone()));\n441\t\n442\t        // Third call should wrap to node-1\n443\t        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n444\t        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n445\t    }\n446\t\n447\t    #[tokio::test]\n448\t    async fn test_record_request_lifecycle() {\n449\t        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n450\t        let node = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n451\t\n452\t        selector.record_request_start(&node).await;\n453\t\n454\t        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n455\t        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n456\t        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 1);\n457\t\n458\t        // Record success decrements in-flight and updates latency\n459\t        selector.record_success(&node, 50.0).await;\n460\t\n461\t        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n462\t        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n463\t        // In-flight should be decremented (from 1 to 0)\n464\t        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 0);\n465\t    }\n466\t\n467\t    #[tokio::test]\n468\t    async fn test_empty_candidates() {\n469\t        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n470\t        let selected = selector.select(&[], 0).await;\n471\t        assert!(selected.is_none());\n472\t    }\n473\t}\n474\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"871da322-e075-4ccf-a33e-4586bb4274a0","uuid":"371b992f-dd6b-496c-8a1f-34c5a0e68f05","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:31:09.036Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/replica_selection.rs","content":"//! Adaptive replica selection using EWMA scoring (plan §13.3).\n//!\n//! Replaces round-robin with latency-aware selection using EWMA-smoothed\n//! metrics: latency p95, in-flight request count, and error rate.\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse rand::prelude::*;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Replica selection strategy.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum SelectionStrategy {\n    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n    Adaptive,\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    RoundRobin,\n    /// Random selection.\n    Random,\n}\n\nimpl Default for SelectionStrategy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Adaptive\n    }\n}\n\n/// Replica selection configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    /// Selection strategy.\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub strategy: String,\n    /// Latency weight in score computation.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_latency_weight\")]\n    pub latency_weight: f64,\n    /// In-flight request weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n    /// Error rate weight.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n    pub error_weight: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life in milliseconds.\n    #[serde(default = \"default_ewma_half_life\")]\n    pub ewma_half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Exploration epsilon (probability of random selection).\n    #[serde(default = \"default_epsilon\")]\n    pub exploration_epsilon: f64,\n}\n\nfn default_latency_weight() -> f64 {\n    1.0\n}\nfn default_inflight_weight() -> f64 {\n    2.0\n}\nfn default_error_weight() -> f64 {\n    10.0\n}\nfn default_ewma_half_life() -> u64 {\n    5000\n}\nfn default_epsilon() -> f64 {\n    0.05\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            strategy: \"adaptive\".into(),\n            latency_weight: default_latency_weight(),\n            inflight_weight: default_inflight_weight(),\n            error_weight: default_error_weight(),\n            ewma_half_life_ms: default_ewma_half_life(),\n            exploration_epsilon: default_epsilon(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Per-node metrics for adaptive selection.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeMetrics {\n    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n    /// Current in-flight request count.\n    pub in_flight: u32,\n    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n    pub error_rate: f64,\n    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n    /// Last update timestamp.\n    pub last_updated: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeMetrics {\n    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n            in_flight: 0,\n            error_rate: 0.0,\n            half_life_ms,\n            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment in-flight count.\n    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight += 1;\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Callback for reporting selection events.\npub trait SelectionObserver: Send + Sync {\n    /// Called when a node is selected with its score.\n    fn report_selection(&self, node_id: &str, score: f64);\n    /// Called when exploration selects a random node.\n    fn report_exploration(&self);\n}\n\n/// No-op observer for when metrics aren't needed.\nstruct NoOpObserver;\nimpl SelectionObserver for NoOpObserver {\n    fn report_selection(&self, _node_id: &str, _score: f64) {}\n    fn report_exploration(&self) {}\n}\n\n/// Replica selector.\npub struct ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Configuration.\n    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    /// Per-node metrics.\n    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n    /// Random number generator.\n    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n    /// Observer for selection events.\n    observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>,\n}\n\nimpl ReplicaSelector {\n    /// Create a new replica selector with a metrics observer.\n    pub fn new_with_observer(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig, observer: Arc<dyn SelectionObserver>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n            observer,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new replica selector without metrics.\n    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::new_with_observer(config, Arc::new(NoOpObserver))\n    }\n\n    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n\n        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n\n        match strategy {\n            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n\n        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n        if self.should_explore() {\n            self.observer.report_exploration();\n            let selected = self.select_random(candidates);\n            if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n                let score = metrics\n                    .get(node)\n                    .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                    .unwrap_or(1000.0);\n                self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), score);\n            }\n            return selected;\n        }\n\n        // Compute scores and collect all nodes with the minimum score\n        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n        let mut best_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = Vec::new();\n\n        for node in candidates {\n            let score = metrics\n                .get(node)\n                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n\n            if score < best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best_nodes.clear();\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            } else if (score - best_score).abs() < 1e-10 {\n                // Scores are essentially equal - add to tie list\n                best_nodes.push(node.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If multiple nodes have the same best score, pick randomly\n        let selected = if best_nodes.len() == 1 {\n            best_nodes.into_iter().next()\n        } else {\n            let idx = self.rng.lock().unwrap().gen_range(0..best_nodes.len());\n            best_nodes.get(idx).cloned()\n        };\n\n        if let Some(ref node) = selected {\n            self.observer.report_selection(node.as_str(), best_score);\n        }\n\n        selected\n    }\n\n    /// Round-robin selection.\n    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Random selection.\n    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n        if candidates.is_empty() {\n            return None;\n        }\n        let idx = self\n            .rng\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n    }\n\n    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n        entry.update_error(false);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed request.\n    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n            entry.update_latency(lat);\n        }\n        entry.update_error(true);\n        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, node: &NodeId) {\n        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n        let entry = metrics\n            .entry(node.clone())\n            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n        entry.increment_in_flight();\n    }\n\n    /// Get metrics for a node.\n    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> Option<NodeMetrics> {\n        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n        metrics.get(node).cloned()\n    }\n\n    /// Parse the strategy from config string.\n    fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n            \"random\" => SelectionStrategy::Random,\n            _ => SelectionStrategy::Adaptive,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Clone for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn clone(&self) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: self.config.clone(),\n            metrics: Arc::clone(&self.metrics),\n            rr_counter: Arc::clone(&self.rr_counter),\n            rng: Arc::clone(&self.rng),\n            observer: Arc::clone(&self.observer),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for ReplicaSelector {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_config_default() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n        assert_eq!(config.strategy, \"adaptive\");\n        assert_eq!(config.latency_weight, 1.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.inflight_weight, 2.0);\n        assert_eq!(config.error_weight, 10.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_score() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(50.0, 5000);\n        assert_eq!(metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default()), 50.0);\n\n        metrics.in_flight = 5;\n        let score = metrics.score(&ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n        // 50 * 1.0 + 5 * 2.0 = 60\n        assert_eq!(score, 60.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_metrics_ewma() {\n        let mut metrics = NodeMetrics::new(100.0, 1000); // Short half-life\n\n        metrics.update_latency(50.0);\n        // Should move toward 50\n        assert!(metrics.latency_p95_ms < 100.0 && metrics.latency_p95_ms > 40.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(true);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate > 0.0);\n\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        // Error rate should decay\n        let rate_before = metrics.error_rate;\n        metrics.update_error(false);\n        assert!(metrics.error_rate < rate_before);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_adaptive() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default());\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        // Seed metrics by recording successful requests\n        selector.record_success(&node1, 10.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(&node2, 100.0).await;\n\n        // Should select node-1 (lower score)\n        let candidates = vec![node2.clone(), node1.clone()];\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_select_round_robin() {\n        let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n            strategy: \"round_robin\".into(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(config);\n\n        let node1 = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n        let node2 = NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string());\n\n        let candidates = vec![node1.clone(), node2.clone()];\n\n        // First call should return node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1.clone()));\n\n        // Second call should return node-2\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node2.clone()));\n\n        // Third call should wrap to node-1\n        let selected = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n        assert_eq!(selected, Some(node1));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_record_request_lifecycle() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let node = NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string());\n\n        selector.record_request_start(&node).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 1);\n\n        // Record success decrements in-flight and updates latency\n        selector.record_success(&node, 50.0).await;\n\n        let metrics = selector.get_metrics(&node).await;\n        assert!(metrics.is_some());\n        // In-flight should be decremented (from 1 to 0)\n        assert_eq!(metrics.unwrap().in_flight, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_empty_candidates() {\n        let selector = ReplicaSelector::default();\n        let selected = selector.select(&[], 0).await;\n        assert!(selected.is_none());\n    }\n}\n","numLines":474,"startLine":1,"totalLines":474}}}

 })\n24\t}\n25\t\n26\t/// Create a test group with 3 nodes.\n27\tfn test_group_3() -> Group {\n28\t    let mut group = Group::new(0);\n29\t   for _ in 0..10 {\n98\t        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n99\t    }\n100\t\n101\t    // Wait for EWMA to propagate (2× half-life = 200ms)\n102\t 200).await;\n106\t\n107\t    let degraded_count1 = *degraded.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n108\t    let degraded_count0 = *degraded.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n109\t    let degraded_count2 = *degraded.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n110\t\n111\t    // Node-1 should receive significantly less traffic\n112\t    // With latency_weight=1.0, node-1 score ~200 vs ~50 for others\n113\t    // Expect node-1 to get <15% of traffic\n114\t    assert!(\n115\t        degraded_count1 < 30,\n116\t        \"degraded node-1 still receiving too much traffic: {}\",\n117\t        degraded_count1\n118\t    );\n119\t\n120\t    // Healthy nodes should receive more traffic\n121\t    assert!(\n122\t        degraded_count0 > 50,\n123\t        \"healthy node-0 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n124\t        degraded_count0\n125\t    );\n126\t    assert!(\n127\t        degraded_count2 > 50,\n128\t        \"healthy node-2 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n129\t        degraded_count2\n130\t    );\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n134\t// P5.3-A2: Node recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to uniform\n135\t// &candidates[1];\n145\t    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n146\t\n147\t    // Initialize with good latency\n148\t    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n149\t    selector.record_success(node1,      \"node-1 should be degraded, got {} selections\",\n165\t        degraded_count1\n166\t    );\n167\t\n168\t    // Clear latency: record good responses for node-1\n169\t    for _ in 0..10 {\n170\t        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n171\t    }\n172\t\n173\t    // Wait for recovery (2× half-life)\n174\t    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n175\t\n176\t    // Run queries after recovery\n177\t    let recovered = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 300).await;\n178\t\n179\t    let recovered_count0 = *recovered.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n180\t    let recovered_count1 = *recovered.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n181\t    let recovered_count2 = *recovered.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n182\t\n183\t    // All nodes should have roughly equal distribution (~1/3 each)\n184\t    let expected = 300 / 3;\n185\t    let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.4) as usize; // 40% tolerance for exploration\n186\t\n187\t    assert!(\n188\t        (recovered_count1 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n189\t        \"node-1 recovered count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n190\t        recovered_count1, expected, tolerance\n191\t    );\n192\t    assert!(\n193\t        (recovered_count0 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n194\t        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {}       \"node-2 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n200\t        recovered_count2, expected, tolerance\n201\t    );\n202\t}\n203\t\n204\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n205\t// P5.3-A3: Exploration ensures degraded node still gets some traffic (ε = 0.05)\n206\t// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n207\t\n208\t#[tokio::test]\n209\tasync fn p5_3_a3_exploration_samples_degraded_node() {\n210\t    let selector = 1000).await;\n234\t\n235\t    let count0 = *dist.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n236\t    let count1 = *dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n237\t    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n238\t\n239\t    println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);\n240\t\n241\t    // Node-2 is severely degraded but should ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n273\t// P5.3-A4: Round-robin fallback mode works identically to Phase 1 baseline\n274\t// let group = test_group_3();\n308\t    let node = &group.nodes()[0];\n309\t\n310\t    // Start with 100ms latency\n311\t    selector.record_success(node, 100.0).await;\n312\t    let metrics1 = P5.3 §13.3 Adaptive replica selection (EWMA-based) acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Tests the EWMA-scored replica selection that replaces round-robin:\n//! - score(node) = α · latency_p95_ms + β · in_flight_count + γ · error_rate\n//! - Router picks lowest-scoring eligible node with probability 1 − ε\n//! - With ε (default 0.05) picks uniformly random for exploration\n\nuse miroir_core::replica_selection::{ReplicaSelectionConfig, ReplicaSelector};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Group, NodeId};\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\n/// Create a test selector with default config.\nfn test_selector() -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig::default())\n}\n\n/// Create a selector with custom half-life for faster test convergence.\nfn fast_test_selector(half_life_ms: u64) -> ReplicaSelector {\n    ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: half_life_ms,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05,\n        ..Default::default()\n    })\n}\n\n/// Create a test group with 3 nodes.\nfn test_group_3() -> Group {\n    let mut group = Group::new(0);\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".to_string()));\n    group.add_node(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".to_string()));\n    group\n}\n\n/// Simulate queries to measure node selection distribution.\nasync fn simulate_selections(\n    selector: &ReplicaSelector,\n    candidates: &[NodeId],\n    group_id: u32,\n    count: usize,\n) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> {\n    let mut distribution = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..count {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(candidates, group_id).await {\n            *distribution.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n    distribution\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A1: Traffic shifts away from degraded node within 2× half-life\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a1_degraded_node_receives_less_traffic() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life for fast test\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize all nodes with similar good latency (50ms)\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Run initial queries to establish baseline\n    let baseline = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    // Verify roughly uniform distribution initially\n    let count0 = *baseline.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *baseline.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *baseline.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Each node should get ~33% (allow 20-45% for randomness)\n    let _expected = 200 / 3;\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count0),\n        \"node-0 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count1),\n        \"node-1 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count1\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (20..=90).contains(&count2),\n        \"node-2 baseline count {} out of expected range 20-90\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Induce degradation on node-1: 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for EWMA to propagate (2× half-life = 200ms)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after degradation\n    let degraded = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count0 = *degraded.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let degraded_count2 = *degraded.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // Node-1 should receive significantly less traffic\n    // With latency_weight=1.0, node-1 score ~200 vs ~50 for others\n    // Expect node-1 to get <15% of traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 30,\n        \"degraded node-1 still receiving too much traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should receive more traffic\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count0 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-0 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count0\n    );\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count2 > 50,\n        \"healthy node-2 not receiving enough traffic: {}\",\n        degraded_count2\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A2: Node recovers after latency clears; distribution returns to uniform\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a2_degraded_node_recovers() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(100); // 100ms half-life\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize with good latency\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node2, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Degrade node-1 with 200ms latency\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 200.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Verify degradation worked\n    let degraded_dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 100).await;\n    let degraded_count1 = *degraded_dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    assert!(\n        degraded_count1 < 20,\n        \"node-1 should be degraded, got {} selections\",\n        degraded_count1\n    );\n\n    // Clear latency: record good responses for node-1\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    // Wait for recovery (2× half-life)\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run queries after recovery\n    let recovered = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 300).await;\n\n    let recovered_count0 = *recovered.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count1 = *recovered.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let recovered_count2 = *recovered.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    // All nodes should have roughly equal distribution (~1/3 each)\n    let expected = 300 / 3;\n    let tolerance = (expected as f64 * 0.4) as usize; // 40% tolerance for exploration\n\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count1 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-1 recovered count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count1, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count0 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count0, expected, tolerance\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (recovered_count2 as isize - expected as isize).abs() <= tolerance as isize,\n        \"node-2 count {} not close to expected {} (tolerance {})\",\n        recovered_count2, expected, tolerance\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A3: Exploration ensures degraded node still gets some traffic (ε = 0.05)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a3_exploration_samples_degraded_node() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.05, // 5% exploration\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Initialize: node-0 and node-1 healthy, node-2 severely degraded\n    for _ in 0..10 {\n        selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n        selector.record_success(node2, 500.0).await; // Very high latency\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // Run 1000 queries\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector, &candidates, 0, 1000).await;\n\n    let count0 = *dist.get(\"node-0\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count1 = *dist.get(\"node-1\").unwrap_or(&0);\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    println!(\"Distribution: node-0={}, node-1={}, node-2={}\", count0, count1, count2);\n\n    // Node-2 is severely degraded but should still get ~5% via exploration\n    // Expected: ~50 selections (5% of 1000), allow range 20-80 for randomness\n    assert!(\n        (20..=80).contains(&count2),\n        \"exploration not working: degraded node-2 got {} selections, expected ~50 (range 20-80)\",\n        count2\n    );\n\n    // Healthy nodes should split the remaining ~95%\n    let healthy_total = count0 + count1;\n    assert!(\n        healthy_total >= 900,\n        \"healthy nodes didn't get enough traffic: {}\",\n        healthy_total\n    );\n\n    // Each healthy node should get roughly half of remaining\n    let expected_healthy = 475; // ~95% / 2\n    let tolerance = 100;\n    assert!(\n        (count0 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-0 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count0, expected_healthy\n    );\n    assert!(\n        (count1 as isize - expected_healthy).abs() <= tolerance,\n        \"node-1 count {} not close to expected {}\",\n        count1, expected_healthy\n    );\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// P5.3-A4: Round-robin fallback mode works identically to Phase 1 baseline\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p5_3_a4_round_robin_fallback() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"round_robin\".to_string(),\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 5000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Test round-robin order\n    let first = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let second = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let third = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n    let fourth = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await;\n\n    // Should cycle through candidates in order\n    assert_eq!(first, candidates.get(0).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(second, candidates.get(1).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(third, candidates.get(2).cloned());\n    assert_eq!(fourth, candidates.get(0).cloned()); // Wrap around\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Additional tests for EWMA behavior\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn ewma_converges_toward_new_values() {\n    let selector = fast_test_selector(50);\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let node = &group.nodes()[0];\n\n    // Start with 100ms latency\n    selector.record_success(node, 100.0).await;\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    assert!(metrics1.is_some());\n    let latency1 = metrics1.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n    assert!((90.0..=110.0).contains(&latency1));\n\n    // Shift to 50ms\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_success(node, 50.0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    let metrics2 = selector.get_metrics(node).await;\n    let latency2 = metrics2.unwrap().latency_p95_ms;\n\n    // Should move toward 50\n    assert!(latency2 < latency1, \"EWMA should decrease toward new value\");\n    assert!(latency2 > 40.0, \"EWMA shouldn't overshoot too much\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn in_flight_count_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Add in-flight requests to node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_request_start(node0).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With inflight_weight=2.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with in-flight requests should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn error_rate_affects_score() {\n    let selector = test_selector();\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n\n    // Both have same latency initially\n    selector.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n\n    // Record errors for node-0\n    for _ in 0..5 {\n        selector.record_error(node0, Some(50.0)).await;\n    }\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n\n    // With error_weight=10.0, node-0 score should be higher\n    let metrics0 = selector.get_metrics(node0).await.unwrap();\n    let metrics1 = selector.get_metrics(node1).await.unwrap();\n\n    let config = ReplicaSelectionConfig::default();\n    let score0 = metrics0.score(&config);\n    let score1 = metrics1.score(&config);\n\n    assert!(\n        score0 > score1,\n        \"node-0 with errors should have higher score: {} > {}\",\n        score0,\n        score1\n    );\n\n    // Verify error_rate is set\n    assert!(metrics0.error_rate > 0.0);\n    assert_eq!(metrics1.error_rate, 0.0);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_exploration_epsilon_configurable() {\n    // Test with epsilon = 0.0 (no exploration)\n    let selector_no_explore = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        exploration_epsilon: 0.0,\n        ewma_half_life_ms: 100,\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    let node0 = &candidates[0];\n    let node1 = &candidates[1];\n    let node2 = &candidates[2];\n\n    // Make node-2 severely degraded\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node0, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node1, 50.0).await;\n    selector_no_explore.record_success(node2, 500.0).await;\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;\n\n    // With no exploration, node-2 should get almost no traffic\n    let dist = simulate_selections(&selector_no_explore, &candidates, 0, 200).await;\n    let count2 = *dist.get(\"node-2\").unwrap_or(&0);\n\n    assert_eq!(\n        count2, 0,\n        \"with epsilon=0.0, degraded node should get zero traffic\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_random_strategy() {\n    let selector = ReplicaSelector::new(ReplicaSelectionConfig {\n        strategy: \"random\".to_string(),\n        ..Default::default()\n    });\n\n    let group = test_group_3();\n    let candidates: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Run many selections to test uniformity\n    let mut counts = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    for _ in 0..1000 {\n        if let Some(node) = selector.select(&candidates, 0).await {\n            *counts.entry(node.as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Each node should get roughly 1/3 of selections\n    let expected = 1000 / 3;\n    for (node, count) in &counts {\n        let diff = (*count as isize - expected as isize).abs();\n        assert!(\n            diff <= 100, // Allow 10% variance\n            \"{}: got {} selections, expected ~{}\",\n            node, count, expected\n        );\n    }\n}\n","numLines":470,"startLine":1,"totalLines":470}}}

Idempotency keys and query coalescing (plan §13.10).\n//!\n//! This module implements:\n//! - Write deduplication via idempotency keys\n//! - Read query coalescing for identical concurrent searches\n\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::{BTreeMap, HashMap};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant, SystemTime};\nuse tokio::sync::{broadcast, RwLock};\n\n/// Canonicalize a JSON value for fingerprinting.\n///\n/// Sorts object keys by name to ensure consistent representation\n/// regardless of original key order. This is important for query\n/// coalescing where `{q: \"test\", limit: 10}` and `{limit: 10, q: \"test\"}`\n/// should be considered identical.\npub fn canonicalize_json(value: &Value) -> String {\n    match value {\n        Value::Object(map) => {\n            let mut sorted = BTreeMap::new();\n            for (k, v) in map {\n                sorted.insert(k.clone(), canonicalize_json(v));\n            }\n            serde_json::to_string(&sorted).unwrap_or_default()\n        }\n        Value::Array(arr) => {\n            let canonical: Vec<String> = arr.iter().map(canonicalize_json).collect();\n            serde_json::to_string(&canonical).unwrap_or_default()\n        }\n        _ => serde_json::to_string(value).unwrap_or_default(),\n    }\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache entry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct IdempotencyEntry {\n    /// SHA256 hash of the request body.\n    pub body_hash: String,\n    /// Miroir task ID returned to the client.\n    pub mtask_id: String,\n    /// Expiration timestamp.\n    pub expires_at: u64,\n}\n\n/// Idempotency cache for write deduplication.\npub struct IdempotencyCache {\n    /// key -> (body_hash, mtask_id, expires_at)\n    cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, IdempotencyEntry>>>,\n    /// Maximum entries (LRU cap).\n    max_entries: usize,\n    /// TTL in seconds.\n    ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl IdempotencyCache {\n    /// Create a new idempotency cache.\n    pub fn new(max_entries: usize, ttl_secs: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            max_entries,\n            ttl_secs,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check for a duplicate write.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Some(mtask_id)` if
